title: Ffi{code:-1} used as logic-error sentinel, not FFI error
description: Several sites (key.rs key_cache, crypto/cmac.rs compute, crypto/ecc.rs verify_digest, crypto/ed25519.rs sign, crypto/curve25519.rs diffie_hellman) return WolfHsmError::Ffi { code: -1, func: "...: unexpected output length" } for conditions detected entirely in Rust, not from a C return code. Using a fake code -1 under the Ffi variant misrepresents what happened. Suggested rewrite: use WolfHsmError::BadArgs { msg } for post-call sanity checks detected in Rust, or add a dedicated ProtocolError { msg } variant if the distinction between arg validation and unexpected server responses matters.
